name: Eldrazi Stampede Chaos
tagline: Build the curve, then swing — Craterhoof turns a wide board into a one-turn table kill
idea: Ramp hard, cheat huge creatures into play with haste, and turn the board sideways. There is no combo to assemble — you build a damage curve and, once the board is wide enough, one alpha strike ends the table.

briefKills.line: Ghalta, Primal Hunger (a wide Eldrazi/power board drops its cost toward GG) → 12/12 trample lands cheap → annihilator + trample close
Wanderer's two hasty cascades + its own 7 power + beater pile → 15–25 turn-of-cast → focus one opponent

openings: No hard counterspells despite blue — combo decks that go off T4–6 pre-empt your T6–8 window.
Cyclonic Rift / Farewell are devastating — thin recursion, a 3–4 turn rebuild.
Politically loud — Annihilator and 12/12 Eldrazi draw the table’s fire; expect to be the early target.
Weakest axes — Durability & Interaction (3/3): the deck dominates the board but answers little.

clock.note: Does not race the pod — a T≤7 decap lands only ~26% of games; the table kill needs Craterhoof, 8 mana, and a wide board at once.
clock.caption: The Craterhoof alpha is the real table kill — trample + X/X across a wide board ends three opponents at once. The Wanderer cascades are the fast decap.

gamePlan: The deck ramps aggressively (19 dedicated ramp pieces — Sol Ring, Arcane Signet, Mana Vault, Ancient Tomb, Talisman, Thran Dynamo, Birds of Paradise, Llanowar Loamspeaker, Lotus Cobra, Sakura-Tribe Elder, Solemn Simulacrum, Tireless Provisioner, Delighted Halfling, Cultivate, Kodama's Reach, Farseek, Nature's Lore, Rampant Growth, Skyshroud Claim) into a wall of high-mana-value threats and cascade/free-cast effects, then chains the chaos to compound triggers.
winLine: Craterhoof alpha over an Eldrazi/ramp board

finishers.name: Craterhoof Behemoth alpha
finishers.note: Build a wide board (Avenger creates a 0/1 Plant token per land; Rampaging Baloths produces 4/4 vanilla Beast tokens on landfall — they don't have trample unless Garruk's Uprising is out; Tendershoot drips Saprolings on **each** upkeep, not just yours) and resolve Craterhoof Behemoth (MV 8 with {5}{G}{G}{G} — note Craterhoof cannot be cascaded into).

finishers.name: Ghalta + Eldrazi pile
finishers.note: Ghalta Primal Hunger costs {10}{G}{G} reduced by total power of creatures you control (floor {G}{G}).

rulings.name: Cascade requires MV strictly less than 8.
rulings.note: Wanderer cannot cascade into the Eldrazi titans (Ulamogs 10/11, Kozileks 10), Craterhoof (8), or Ghalta (12) — those must be hardcast. It can hit Avenger (7), Etali (6), Selvala's Stampede (6), and lower.

rulings.name: Sunbird's Invocation triggers ONLY on spells cast from hand
rulings.note: cascade hits, Aetherworks / Etali / Creative Technique free-casts, and Selvala's Stampede ETBs do NOT trigger it.

rulings.name: Both Ulamogs are indestructible
rulings.note: (survive non-exile wipes). Ulamog Infinite Gyre and Kozilek Butcher self-shuffle from the graveyard; Ulamog Ceaseless Hunger does NOT self-recur — once milled or exiled it's gone, so hardcast it or keep it in hand.

rulings.name: Void Winnower
rulings.note: while it's on the battlefield, opponents can't cast even-MV spells (2/4/6 removal and counters) AND can't block with even-MV creatures.

rulings.name: Conduit of Worlds is one-spell-per-turn-total
rulings.note: when you grave-cast (not a bonus cast), and only at sorcery speed if you haven't cast a spell yet that turn.

rulings.name: Creative Technique's Demonstrate lets an opponent copy it too
Decimate needs a legal target in all four categories
rulings.note: (artifact + creature + enchantment + land) or it can't be cast.

mull.strategy: Looking for: ramp that reaches 6+ mana by T4–5, plus a payoff or an engine piece (Wanderer, Conduit of Ruin, Sunbird's). Two ramp pieces beat one. The classic trap keep is 4–5 lands plus only 8-drops — no ramp, no early engine; ship it even though the land count looks safe.

killTree.title: Eldrazi Stampede Chaos — Maelstrom Wanderer
killTree.root:
Maelstrom Wanderer cast (5GUR)
two cascades into nonland MV<8 + creatures you control have haste
killTree.stall:
re-ramp toward the 8-mana Wanderer cast and a wide board —
no passive drain; the Craterhoof table-alpha is what closes (~T12)
killTree.caption: a curve, not a tree — ramp the board, then alpha

lines.need:
Ghalta, Primal Hunger
(a wide Eldrazi/power board drops its cost toward GG)
lines.kill: 12/12 trample lands cheap → annihilator + trample close

lines.need:
Wanderer's two hasty cascades
+ its own 7 power + beater pile

Craterhoof Behemoth (8 mana, haste)
+ 8 creatures on board
lines.kill: +X/+X & TRAMPLE → swing distributes across the table

lines.need:
Sunbird's Invocation resolved
then hardcast a big Eldrazi from hand
lines.kill: free chained spells off each cast → snowball to lethal

lines.need:
Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger hardcast (10 mana)
(fallback)
lines.kill: attacks exile 20 cards → mill out (uncounterable back-up)
